content.problemStatement:
Given A and B, compute the sum of lcm(a, b) over all pairs of positive integers a and b such that:


(1)  a<=A and b<=B. 

(2) There is no integer n>1 such that n2 divides both a and b. 


Give your answer modulo 230.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two space-separated integers A and B.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the sum of lcm(a, b) over all pairs of positive integers a and b such that a <= A and b <= B, and there is no integer n > 1 such that n^2 divides both a and b, modulo 2^30.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case, we calculate the sum of lcm(a, b) over all pairs of positive integers a and b such that a <= A and b <= B, and there is no integer n > 1 such that n^2 divides both a and b.
There is only one pair (1, 1) and lcm(1, 1) = 1.
The pairs are (1, 1), (1, 2), (2, 1), and (2, 2). The sum of their least common multiples is 1 + 2 + 2 + 0 = 5.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can iterate over all pairs of positive integers a and b such that a <= A and b <= B. For each pair, we check if there is no integer n > 1 such that n^2 divides both a and b. If the condition is met, we calculate the least common multiple of a and b and add it to the sum. Finally, we print the sum modulo 2^30.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * A * B) per test case, O(T * A * B) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the space used does not grow with the size of the input.
